net.JOB_8_20: “Surely, God does not reject a blameless man, nor does he grasp the hand of the evildoers.
net.JOB_8_21: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with gladness.
net.JOB_8_22: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.”
net.JOB_9_2: “Truly, I know that this is so. But how can a human be just before God?
net.JOB_9_3: If someone wishes to contend with him, he cannot answer him one time in a thousand.
net.JOB_9_4: He is wise in heart and mighty in strength — who has resisted him and remained safe?
net.JOB_9_5: He who removes mountains suddenly, who overturns them in his anger,
net.JOB_9_6: he who shakes the earth out of its place so that its pillars tremble,
net.JOB_9_7: he who commands the sun, and it does not shine and seals up the stars,
net.JOB_9_8: he alone spreads out the heavens, and treads on the waves of the sea.
net.JOB_9_9: He makes the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, and the constellations of the southern sky;
net.JOB_9_10: he does great and unsearchable things, and wonderful things without number.
net.JOB_9_11: If he passes by me, I cannot see him, if he goes by, I cannot perceive him.
net.JOB_9_12: If he snatches away, who can turn him back? Who dares to say to him, ‘What are you doing?’
net.JOB_9_13: God does not restrain his anger; under him the helpers of Rahab lie crushed. The Impossibility of Facing God in Court
net.JOB_9_14: “How much less, then, can I answer him and choose my words to argue with him.
net.JOB_9_15: Although I am innocent, I could not answer him; I could only plead with my judge for mercy.
net.JOB_9_16: If I summoned him, and he answered me, I would not believe that he would be listening to my voice—
net.JOB_9_17: he who crushes me with a tempest, and multiplies my wounds for no reason.
net.JOB_9_18: He does not allow me to recover my breath, for he fills me with bitterness.
net.JOB_9_19: If it is a matter of strength, most certainly he is the strong one! And if it is a matter of justice, he will say, ‘Who will summon me?’
net.JOB_9_20: Although I am innocent, my mouth would condemn me, although I am blameless, it would declare me perverse.
net.JOB_9_21: I am blameless. I do not know myself. I despise my life. Accusation of God’s Justice
net.JOB_9_22: “It is all one! That is why I say, ‘He destroys the blameless and the guilty.’
net.JOB_9_23: If a scourge brings sudden death, he mocks at the despair of the innocent.
net.JOB_9_24: If a land has been given into the hand of a wicked man, he covers the faces of its judges; if it is not he, then who is it? Renewed Complaint
net.JOB_9_25: “My days are swifter than a runner, they speed by without seeing happiness.
net.JOB_9_26: They glide by like reed boats, like an eagle that swoops down on its prey.
net.JOB_9_27: If I say, ‘I will forget my complaint, I will change my expression and be cheerful,’
net.JOB_9_28: I dread all my sufferings, for I know that you do not hold me blameless.
net.JOB_9_29: If I am guilty, why then weary myself in vain?
net.JOB_9_30: If I wash myself with snow-melt water, and make my hands clean with lye,
net.JOB_9_31: then you plunge me into a slimy pit and my own clothes abhor me.
net.JOB_9_32: For he is not a human being like I am, that I might answer him, that we might come together in judgment.
net.JOB_9_33: Nor is there an arbiter between us, who might lay his hand on us both,
net.JOB_9_34: who would take his rod away from me so that his terror would not make me afraid.
net.JOB_9_35: Then would I speak and not fear him, but it is not so with me.
net.JOB_10_1: “I am weary of my life; I will complain freely without restraint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
net.JOB_10_2: I will say to God, ‘Do not condemn me; tell me why you are contending with me.’
net.JOB_10_3: Is it good for you to oppress, to despise the work of your hands, while you smile on the schemes of the wicked? Motivations of God
net.JOB_10_4: “Do you have eyes of flesh, or do you see as a human being sees?
net.JOB_10_5: Are your days like the days of a mortal, or your years like the years of a mortal,
net.JOB_10_6: that you must search out my iniquity, and inquire about my sin,
net.JOB_10_7: although you know that I am not guilty, and that there is no one who can deliver out of your hand? Contradictions in God’s Dealings
net.JOB_10_8: “Your hands have shaped me and made me, but now you destroy me completely.
net.JOB_10_9: Remember that you have made me as with the clay; will you return me to dust?
net.JOB_10_10: Did you not pour me out like milk, and curdle me like cheese?
net.JOB_10_11: You clothed me with skin and flesh and knit me together with bones and sinews.
net.JOB_10_12: You gave me life and favor, and your intervention watched over my spirit.
net.JOB_10_13: “But these things you have concealed in your heart; I know that this is with you:
net.JOB_10_14: If I sinned, then you would watch me and you would not acquit me of my iniquity.
net.JOB_10_15: If I am guilty, woe to me, and if I am innocent, I cannot lift my head; I am full of shame, and satiated with my affliction.
net.JOB_10_16: If I lift myself up, you hunt me as a fierce lion, and again you display your power against me.
net.JOB_10_17: You bring new witnesses against me, and increase your anger against me; relief troops come against me. An Appeal for Relief
net.JOB_10_18: “Why then did you bring me out from the womb? I should have died and no eye would have seen me!
net.JOB_10_19: I should have been as though I had never existed; I should have been carried right from the womb to the grave!
net.JOB_10_20: Are not my days few? Cease, then, and leave me alone that I may find a little comfort,
net.JOB_10_21: before I depart, never to return, to the land of darkness and the deepest shadow,
net.JOB_10_22: to the land of utter darkness, like the deepest darkness, and the deepest shadow and disorder, where even the light is like darkness.”
net.JOB_11_1: Then Zophar the Naamathite spoke up and said:
net.JOB_11_2: “Should not this abundance of words be answered, or should this talkative man be vindicated?
net.JOB_11_3: Should people remain silent at your idle talk, and should no one rebuke you when you mock?
net.JOB_11_4: For you have said, ‘My teaching is flawless, and I am pure in your sight.’
net.JOB_11_5: But if only God would speak, if only he would open his lips against you,
net.JOB_11_6: and reveal to you the secrets of wisdom— for true wisdom has two sides — so that you would know that God has forgiven some of your sins.
net.JOB_11_7: “Can you discover the essence of God? Can you find out the perfection of the Almighty?
net.JOB_11_8: It is higher than the heavens—what can you do? It is deeper than Sheol —what can you know?
net.JOB_11_9: Its measure is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.
net.JOB_11_10: If he comes by and confines you and convenes a court, then who can prevent him?
net.JOB_11_11: For he knows deceitful men; when he sees evil, will he not consider it?
net.JOB_11_12: But an empty man will become wise, when a wild donkey’s colt is born a human being.
net.JOB_11_13: “As for you, if you prove faithful, and if you stretch out your hands toward him,
net.JOB_11_14: if iniquity is in your hand—put it far away, and do not let evil reside in your tents.
net.JOB_11_15: For then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be securely established and will not fear.
net.JOB_11_16: For you will forget your trouble; you will remember it like water that has flowed away.
net.JOB_11_17: And life will be brighter than the noonday; though there be darkness, it will be like the morning.
net.JOB_11_18: And you will be secure, because there is hope; you will be protected and will take your rest in safety.
net.JOB_11_19: You will lie down with no one to make you afraid, and many will seek your favor.
net.JOB_11_20: But the eyes of the wicked fail, and escape eludes them; their one hope is to breathe their last.”
net.JOB_12_2: “Without a doubt you are the people, and wisdom will die with you.
net.JOB_12_3: I also have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
net.JOB_12_4: I am a laughingstock to my friends, I, who called on God and whom he answered — a righteous and blameless man is a laughingstock!
net.JOB_12_5: For calamity, there is derision (according to the ideas of the fortunate)— a fate for those whose feet slip.
net.JOB_12_6: But the tents of robbers are peaceful, and those who provoke God are confident — who carry their god in their hands. Knowledge of God’s Wisdom
net.JOB_12_7: “But now, ask the animals and they will teach you, or the birds of the sky and they will tell you.
net.JOB_12_8: Or speak to the earth and it will teach you, or let the fish of the sea declare to you.
net.JOB_12_9: Which of all these does not know that the hand of the Lord has done this?
net.JOB_12_10: In his hand is the life of every creature and the breath of all the human race.
net.JOB_12_11: Does not the ear test words, as the tongue tastes food?
net.JOB_12_12: Is not wisdom found among the aged? Does not long life bring understanding?
net.JOB_12_13: “With God are wisdom and power; counsel and understanding are his.
net.JOB_12_14: If he tears down, it cannot be rebuilt; if he imprisons a person, there is no escape.
net.JOB_12_15: If he holds back the waters, then they dry up; if he releases them, they destroy the land.
net.JOB_12_16: With him are strength and prudence; both the one who goes astray and the one who misleads are his.
net.JOB_12_17: He leads counselors away stripped and makes judges into fools.
net.JOB_12_18: He loosens the bonds of kings and binds a loincloth around their waist.
net.JOB_12_19: He leads priests away stripped and overthrows the potentates.
net.JOB_12_20: He deprives the trusted advisers of speech and takes away the discernment of elders.
net.JOB_12_21: He pours contempt on noblemen and disarms the powerful.
net.JOB_12_22: He reveals the deep things of darkness, and brings deep shadows into the light.
net.JOB_12_23: He makes nations great, and destroys them; he extends the boundaries of nations and disperses them.
net.JOB_12_24: He deprives the leaders of the earth of their understanding; he makes them wander in a trackless desert waste.
net.JOB_12_25: They grope about in darkness without light; he makes them stagger like drunkards.
net.JOB_13_1: “Indeed, my eyes have seen all this, my ears have heard and understood it.
net.JOB_13_2: What you know, I know also; I am not inferior to you!
net.JOB_13_3: But I wish to speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.
net.JOB_13_4: But you, however, are inventors of lies; all of you are worthless physicians!
net.JOB_13_5: If only you would keep completely silent! For you, that would be wisdom.
net.JOB_13_6: “Listen now to my argument, and be attentive to my lips’ contentions.
net.JOB_13_7: Will you speak wickedly on God’s behalf? Will you speak deceitfully for him?
net.JOB_13_8: Will you show him partiality? Will you argue the case for God?
net.JOB_13_9: Would it turn out well if he would examine you? Or as one deceives a man would you deceive him?
net.JOB_13_10: He would certainly rebuke you if you secretly showed partiality.
net.JOB_13_11: Would not his splendor terrify you and the fear he inspires fall on you?
net.JOB_13_12: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
net.JOB_13_13: “Refrain from talking with me so that I may speak; then let come to me what may.
net.JOB_13_14: Why do I put myself in peril, and take my life in my hands?
net.JOB_13_15: Even if he slays me, I will hope in him; I will surely defend my ways to his face.
net.JOB_13_16: Moreover, this will become my deliverance, for no godless person would come before him.
net.JOB_13_17: Listen carefully to my words; let your ears be attentive to my explanation.
net.JOB_13_18: See now, I have prepared my case; I know that I am right.
net.JOB_13_19: Who will contend with me? If anyone can, I will be silent and die.
net.JOB_13_20: Only in two things spare me, O God, and then I will not hide from your face:
net.JOB_13_21: Remove your hand far from me and stop making me afraid with your terror.
net.JOB_13_22: Then call, and I will answer, or I will speak, and you respond to me.
net.JOB_13_23: How many are my iniquities and sins? Show me my transgression and my sin.
net.JOB_13_24: Why do you hide your face and regard me as your enemy?
net.JOB_13_25: Do you wish to torment a windblown leaf and chase after dry chaff?
net.JOB_13_26: For you write down bitter things against me and cause me to inherit the sins of my youth.
net.JOB_13_27: And you put my feet in the stocks and you watch all my movements; you put marks on the soles of my feet.
net.JOB_13_28: So I waste away like something rotten, like a garment eaten by moths.
net.JOB_14_1: “Man, born of woman, lives but a few days, and they are full of trouble.
net.JOB_14_2: He grows up like a flower and then withers away; he flees like a shadow, and does not remain.
net.JOB_14_3: Do you fix your eye on such a one? And do you bring me before you for judgment?
net.JOB_14_4: Who can make a clean thing come from an unclean? No one!
net.JOB_14_5: Since man’s days are determined, the number of his months is under your control; you have set his limit and he cannot pass it.
net.JOB_14_6: Look away from him and let him desist, until he fulfills his time like a hired man. The Inevitability of Death
net.JOB_14_7: “But there is hope for a tree: If it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its new shoots will not fail.
net.JOB_14_8: Although its roots may grow old in the ground and its stump begins to die in the soil,
net.JOB_14_9: at the scent of water it will flourish and put forth shoots like a new plant.
net.JOB_14_10: But man dies and is powerless; he expires—and where is he?
net.JOB_14_11: As water disappears from the sea, or a river drains away and dries up,
net.JOB_14_12: so man lies down and does not rise; until the heavens are no more, they will not awake nor arise from their sleep. The Possibility of Another Life
net.JOB_14_13: “O that you would hide me in Sheol, and conceal me till your anger has passed! O that you would set me a time and then remember me!
net.JOB_14_14: If a man dies, will he live again? All the days of my hard service I will wait until my release comes.
net.JOB_14_15: You will call and I —I will answer you; you will long for the creature you have made. The Present Condition
net.JOB_14_16: “Surely now you count my steps; then you would not mark my sin.
net.JOB_14_17: My offenses would be sealed up in a bag; you would cover over my sin.
net.JOB_14_18: But as a mountain falls away and crumbles, and as a rock will be removed from its place,
net.JOB_14_19: as water wears away stones, and torrents wash away the soil, so you destroy man’s hope.
net.JOB_14_20: You overpower him once for all, and he departs; you change his appearance and send him away.
net.JOB_14_21: If his sons are honored, he does not know it; if they are brought low, he does not see it.
net.JOB_14_22: His flesh only has pain for him, and he mourns for himself.”
net.JOB_15_2: “Does a wise man answer with blustery knowledge, or fill his belly with the east wind?
net.JOB_15_3: Does he argue with useless talk, with words that have no value in them?
net.JOB_15_4: But you even break off piety, and hinder meditation before God.
net.JOB_15_5: Your sin inspires your mouth; you choose the language of the crafty.
net.JOB_15_6: Your own mouth condemns you, not I; your own lips testify against you.
net.JOB_15_7: “Were you the first man ever born? Were you brought forth before the hills?
net.JOB_15_8: Do you listen in on God’s secret council? Do you limit wisdom to yourself?
net.JOB_15_9: What do you know that we don’t know? What do you understand that we don’t understand?
net.JOB_15_10: The gray-haired and the aged are on our side, men far older than your father.
net.JOB_15_11: Are God’s consolations too trivial for you, or a word spoken in gentleness to you?
net.JOB_15_12: Why has your heart carried you away, and why do your eyes flash,
net.JOB_15_13: when you turn your rage against God and allow such words to escape from your mouth?
net.JOB_15_14: What is man that he should be pure, or one born of woman, that he should be righteous?
net.JOB_15_15: If God places no trust in his holy ones, if even the heavens are not pure in his eyes,
net.JOB_15_16: how much less man, who is abominable and corrupt, who drinks in evil like water!
net.JOB_15_17: “I will explain to you; listen to me, and what I have seen, I will declare,
net.JOB_15_18: what wise men declare, hiding nothing, from the tradition of their ancestors,
net.JOB_15_19: to whom alone the land was given when no foreigner passed among them.
net.JOB_15_20: All his days the wicked man suffers torment, throughout the number of the years that are stored up for the tyrant.
net.JOB_15_21: Terrifying sounds fill his ears; in a time of peace marauders attack him.
net.JOB_15_22: He does not expect to escape from darkness; he is marked for the sword;
net.JOB_15_23: he wanders about—food for vultures — he knows that the day of darkness is at hand.
net.JOB_15_24: Distress and anguish terrify him; they prevail against him like a king ready to launch an attack,
net.JOB_15_25: for he stretches out his hand against God, and vaunts himself against the Almighty,
net.JOB_15_26: defiantly charging against him with a thick, strong shield!
net.JOB_15_27: Because he covered his face with fat, and made his hips bulge with fat,
net.JOB_15_28: he lived in ruined towns and in houses where no one lives, where they are ready to crumble into heaps.
net.JOB_15_29: He will not grow rich, and his wealth will not endure, nor will his possessions spread over the land.
net.JOB_15_30: He will not escape the darkness; a flame will wither his shoots and he will depart by the breath of God’s mouth.
net.JOB_15_31: Let him not trust in what is worthless, deceiving himself; for worthlessness will be his reward.
net.JOB_15_32: Before his time he will be paid in full, and his branches will not flourish.
net.JOB_15_33: Like a vine he will let his sour grapes fall, and like an olive tree he will shed his blossoms.
net.JOB_15_34: For the company of the godless is barren, and fire consumes the tents of those who accept bribes.
net.JOB_15_35: They conceive trouble and bring forth evil; their belly prepares deception.”
net.JOB_16_2: “I have heard many things like these before. What miserable comforters are you all!
net.JOB_16_3: Will there be an end to your windy words? Or what provokes you that you answer?
net.JOB_16_4: I also could speak like you, if you were in my place; I could pile up words against you and I could shake my head at you.
net.JOB_16_5: But I would strengthen you with my words; comfort from my lips would bring you relief. Abandonment by God and Man
net.JOB_16_6: “But if I speak, my pain is not relieved, and if I refrain from speaking, how much of it goes away?
net.JOB_16_7: Surely now he has worn me out, you have devastated my entire household.
net.JOB_16_8: You have seized me, and it has become a witness; my leanness has risen up against me and testifies against me.
net.JOB_16_9: His anger has torn me and persecuted me; he has gnashed at me with his teeth; my adversary locks his eyes on me.
net.JOB_16_10: People have opened their mouths against me, they have struck my cheek in scorn; they unite together against me.
net.JOB_16_11: God abandons me to evil men, and throws me into the hands of wicked men.
net.JOB_16_12: I was in peace, and he has shattered me. He has seized me by the neck and crushed me. He has made me his target;
net.JOB_16_13: his archers surround me. Without pity he pierces my kidneys and pours out my gall on the ground.
net.JOB_16_14: He breaks through against me, time and time again; he rushes against me like a warrior.
net.JOB_16_15: I have sewed sackcloth on my skin, and buried my horn in the dust;
net.JOB_16_16: my face is reddened because of weeping, and on my eyelids there is a deep darkness,
net.JOB_16_17: although there is no violence in my hands and my prayer is pure. An Appeal to God as Witness
net.JOB_16_18: “O earth, do not cover my blood, nor let there be a secret place for my cry.
net.JOB_16_19: Even now my witness is in heaven; my advocate is on high.
net.JOB_16_20: My intercessor is my friend as my eyes pour out tears to God;
net.JOB_16_21: and he contends with God on behalf of man as a man pleads for his friend.
net.JOB_16_22: For the years that lie ahead are few, and then I will go on the way of no return.
net.JOB_17_1: My spirit is broken, my days have faded out, the grave awaits me.
net.JOB_17_2: Surely mockery is with me; my eyes must dwell on their hostility.
net.JOB_17_3: Set my pledge beside you. Who else will put up security for me?
net.JOB_17_4: Because you have closed their minds to understanding, therefore you will not exalt them.
net.JOB_17_5: If a man denounces his friends for personal gain, the eyes of his children will fail.
net.JOB_17_6: He has made me a byword to people, I am the one in whose face they spit.
net.JOB_17_7: My eyes have grown dim with grief; my whole frame is but a shadow.
net.JOB_17_8: Upright men are appalled at this; the innocent man is troubled with the godless.
net.JOB_17_9: But the righteous man holds to his way, and the one with clean hands grows stronger. Anticipation of Death
net.JOB_17_10: “But turn, all of you, and come now! I will not find a wise man among you.
net.JOB_17_11: My days have passed, my plans are shattered, even the desires of my heart.
net.JOB_17_12: These men change night into day; they say, ‘The light is near in the face of darkness.’
net.JOB_17_13: If I hope for the grave to be my home, if I spread out my bed in darkness,
net.JOB_17_14: if I cry out to corruption, ‘You are my father,’ and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ or ‘My sister,’
net.JOB_17_15: where then is my hope? And my hope, who sees it?
net.JOB_17_16: Will it go down to the barred gates of death? Will we descend together into the dust?”
net.JOB_18_2: “How long until you make an end of words? You must consider, and then we can talk.
net.JOB_18_3: Why should we be regarded as beasts, and considered stupid in your sight?
net.JOB_18_4: You who tear yourself to pieces in your anger, will the earth be abandoned for your sake? Or will a rock be moved from its place?
net.JOB_18_5: “Yes, the lamp of the wicked is extinguished; his flame of fire does not shine.
net.JOB_18_6: The light in his tent grows dark; his lamp above him is extinguished.
net.JOB_18_7: His vigorous steps are restricted, and his own counsel throws him down.
net.JOB_18_8: For he has been thrown into a net by his feet and he wanders into a mesh.
net.JOB_18_9: A trap seizes him by the heel; a snare grips him.
net.JOB_18_10: A rope is hidden for him on the ground and a trap for him lies on the path.
net.JOB_18_11: Terrors frighten him on all sides and dog his every step.
net.JOB_18_12: Calamity is hungry for him, and misfortune is ready at his side.
net.JOB_18_13: It eats away parts of his skin; the most terrible death devours his limbs.
net.JOB_18_14: He is dragged from the security of his tent, and marched off to the king of terrors.
net.JOB_18_15: Fire resides in his tent; over his residence burning sulfur is scattered.
net.JOB_18_16: Below his roots dry up, and his branches wither above.
net.JOB_18_17: His memory perishes from the earth, he has no name in the land.
net.JOB_18_18: He is driven from light into darkness and is banished from the world.
net.JOB_18_19: He has neither children nor descendants among his people, no survivor in those places he once stayed.
net.JOB_18_20: People of the west are appalled at his fate; people of the east are seized with horror, saying,
net.JOB_18_21: ‘Surely such is the residence of an evil man; and this is the place of one who has not known God.’”
net.JOB_19_2: “How long will you torment me and crush me with your words?
net.JOB_19_3: These ten times you have been reproaching me; you are not ashamed to attack me.
net.JOB_19_4: But even if it were true that I have erred, my error remains solely my concern!
net.JOB_19_5: If indeed you would exalt yourselves above me and plead my disgrace against me,
net.JOB_19_6: know then that God has wronged me and encircled me with his net. Job’s Abandonment and Affliction
net.JOB_19_7: “If I cry out, ‘Violence!’ I receive no answer; I cry for help, but there is no justice.
net.JOB_19_8: He has blocked my way so I cannot pass, and has set darkness over my paths.
net.JOB_19_9: He has stripped me of my honor and has taken the crown off my head.
net.JOB_19_10: He tears me down on every side until I perish; he uproots my hope like an uprooted tree.
net.JOB_19_11: Thus his anger burns against me, and he considers me among his enemies.
net.JOB_19_12: His troops advance together; they throw up a siege ramp against me, and they camp around my tent. Job’s Forsaken State
net.JOB_19_13: “He has put my relatives far from me; my acquaintances only turn away from me.
net.JOB_19_14: My kinsmen have failed me; my friends have forgotten me.
net.JOB_19_15: My guests and my servant girls consider me a stranger; I am a foreigner in their eyes.
net.JOB_19_16: I summon my servant, but he does not respond, even though I implore him with my own mouth.
net.JOB_19_17: My breath is repulsive to my wife; I am loathsome to my brothers.
net.JOB_19_18: Even youngsters have scorned me; when I get up, they scoff at me.
net.JOB_19_19: All my closest friends detest me; and those whom I love have turned against me.
net.JOB_19_20: My bones stick to my skin and my flesh; I have escaped alive with only the skin of my teeth.
net.JOB_19_21: Have pity on me, my friends, have pity on me, for the hand of God has struck me.
net.JOB_19_22: Why do you pursue me like God does? Will you never be satiated with my flesh? Job’s Assurance of Vindication
net.JOB_19_23: “O that my words were written down! O that they were written on a scroll!
net.JOB_19_24: O that with an iron chisel and with lead they were engraved in a rock forever!
net.JOB_19_25: As for me, I know that my Redeemer lives, and that as the last he will stand upon the earth.
net.JOB_19_26: And after my skin has been destroyed, yet in my flesh I will see God,
net.JOB_19_27: whom I will see for myself, and whom my own eyes will behold, and not another. My heart grows faint within me.
net.JOB_19_28: If you say, ‘How we will pursue him, since the root of the trouble is found in him!’
net.JOB_19_29: Fear the sword yourselves, for wrath brings the punishment by the sword, so that you may know that there is judgment.”
net.JOB_20_2: “This is why my troubled thoughts bring me back — because of my feelings within me.
net.JOB_20_3: When I hear a reproof that dishonors me, then my understanding prompts me to answer.
net.JOB_20_4: “Surely you know that it has been from old, ever since humankind was placed on the earth,
net.JOB_20_5: that the elation of the wicked is brief, the joy of the godless lasts but a moment.
net.JOB_20_6: Even though his stature reaches to the heavens and his head touches the clouds,
net.JOB_20_7: he will perish forever, like his own excrement; those who used to see him will say, ‘Where is he?’
net.JOB_20_8: Like a dream he flies away, never again to be found, and like a vision of the night he is put to flight.
net.JOB_20_9: People who had seen him will not see him again, and the place where he was will recognize him no longer.
net.JOB_20_10: His sons must recompense the poor; his own hands must return his wealth.
net.JOB_20_11: His bones were full of his youthful vigor, but that vigor will lie down with him in the dust.
net.JOB_20_12: “If evil is sweet in his mouth and he hides it under his tongue,
net.JOB_20_13: if he retains it for himself and does not let it go, and holds it fast in his mouth,
net.JOB_20_14: his food is turned sour in his stomach; it becomes the venom of serpents within him.
net.JOB_20_15: The wealth that he consumed he vomits up, God will make him throw it out of his stomach.
net.JOB_20_16: He sucks the poison of serpents; the fangs of a viper kill him.
net.JOB_20_17: He will not look on the streams, the rivers that are the torrents of honey and butter.
net.JOB_20_18: He gives back the ill-gotten gain without assimilating it; he will not enjoy the wealth from his commerce.
net.JOB_20_19: For he has oppressed the poor and abandoned them; he has seized a house which he did not build.
net.JOB_20_20: For he knows no satisfaction in his appetite; he does not let anything he desires escape.
net.JOB_20_21: “Nothing is left for him to devour; that is why his prosperity does not last.
net.JOB_20_22: In the fullness of his sufficiency, distress overtakes him. The full force of misery will come upon him.
net.JOB_20_23: “While he is filling his belly, God sends his burning anger against him, and rains down his blows upon him.
net.JOB_20_24: If he flees from an iron weapon, then an arrow from a bronze bow pierces him.
net.JOB_20_25: When he pulls it out and it comes out of his back, the gleaming point out of his liver, terrors come over him.
net.JOB_20_26: Total darkness waits to receive his treasures; a fire that has not been kindled will consume him and devour what is left in his tent.
net.JOB_20_27: The heavens reveal his iniquity; the earth rises up against him.
net.JOB_20_28: A flood will carry off his house, rushing waters on the day of God’s wrath.
net.JOB_20_29: Such is the lot God allots the wicked, and the heritage of his appointment from God.”
net.JOB_21_2: “Listen carefully to my words; let this be the consolation you offer me.
net.JOB_21_3: Bear with me and I will speak, and after I have spoken you may mock.
net.JOB_21_4: Is my complaint against a man? If so, why should I not be impatient?
net.JOB_21_5: Look at me and be appalled; put your hands over your mouths.
net.JOB_21_6: For, when I think about this, I am terrified and my body feels a shudder. The Wicked Prosper
net.JOB_21_7: “Why do the wicked go on living, grow old, even increase in power?
net.JOB_21_8: Their children are firmly established in their presence, their offspring before their eyes.
net.JOB_21_9: Their houses are safe and without fear; and no rod of punishment from God is upon them.
net.JOB_21_10: Their bulls breed without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
net.JOB_21_11: They allow their children to run like a flock; their little ones dance about.
net.JOB_21_12: They sing to the accompaniment of tambourine and harp, and make merry to the sound of the flute.
net.JOB_21_13: They live out their years in prosperity and go down to the grave in peace.
net.JOB_21_14: So they say to God, ‘Turn away from us! We do not want to know your ways.
net.JOB_21_15: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What would we gain if we were to pray to him?’
net.JOB_21_16: But their prosperity is not their own doing. The counsel of the wicked is far from me! How Often Do the Wicked Suffer?
net.JOB_21_17: “How often is the lamp of the wicked extinguished? How often does their misfortune come upon them? How often does God apportion pain to them in his anger?
net.JOB_21_18: How often are they like straw before the wind, and like chaff swept away by a whirlwind?
net.JOB_21_19: You may say, ‘God stores up a man’s punishment for his children!’ Instead let him repay the man himself so that he may be humbled!
net.JOB_21_20: Let his own eyes see his destruction; let him drink of the anger of the Almighty.
net.JOB_21_21: For what is his interest in his home after his death, when the number of his months has been broken off?
net.JOB_21_22: Can anyone teach God knowledge, since he judges those that are on high? Death Levels Everything
net.JOB_21_23: “One man dies in his full vigor, completely secure and prosperous,
net.JOB_21_24: his body well nourished, and the marrow of his bones moist.
net.JOB_21_25: And another man dies in bitterness of soul, never having tasted anything good.
net.JOB_21_26: Together they lie down in the dust, and worms cover over them both. Futile Words, Deceptive Answers
net.JOB_21_27: “Yes, I know what you are thinking, the schemes by which you would wrong me.
net.JOB_21_28: For you say, ‘Where now is the nobleman’s house, and where are the tents in which the wicked lived?’
net.JOB_21_29: Have you never questioned those who travel the roads? Do you not recognize their accounts —
net.JOB_21_30: that the evil man is spared from the day of his misfortune, that he is delivered from the day of God’s wrath?
net.JOB_21_31: No one denounces his conduct to his face; no one repays him for what he has done.
net.JOB_21_32: And when he is carried to the tombs, and watch is kept over the funeral mound,
net.JOB_21_33: The clods of the torrent valley are sweet to him; behind him everybody follows in procession, and before him goes a countless throng.
net.JOB_21_34: So how can you console me with your futile words? Nothing is left of your answers but deception!”
net.JOB_22_2: “Is it to God that a strong man is of benefit? Is it to him that even a wise man is profitable?
net.JOB_22_3: Is it of any special benefit to the Almighty that you should be righteous, or is it any gain to him that you make your ways blameless?
net.JOB_22_4: Is it because of your piety that he rebukes you and goes to judgment with you?
net.JOB_22_5: Is not your wickedness great and is there no end to your iniquity?
net.JOB_22_6: “For you took pledges from your brothers for no reason, and you stripped the clothing from the naked.
net.JOB_22_7: You gave the weary no water to drink and from the hungry you withheld food.
net.JOB_22_8: Although you were a powerful man, owning land, an honored man living on it,
net.JOB_22_9: you sent widows away empty-handed, and the arms of the orphans you crushed.
net.JOB_22_10: That is why snares surround you, and why sudden fear terrifies you,
net.JOB_22_11: why it is so dark you cannot see, and why a flood of water covers you.
net.JOB_22_12: “Is not God on high in heaven? And see the lofty stars, how high they are!
net.JOB_22_13: But you have said, ‘What does God know? Does he judge through such deep darkness?
net.JOB_22_14: Thick clouds are a veil for him, so he does not see us, as he goes back and forth in the vault of heaven.’
net.JOB_22_15: Will you keep to the old path that evil men have walked—
net.JOB_22_16: men who were carried off before their time, when the flood was poured out on their foundations?
net.JOB_22_17: They were saying to God, ‘Turn away from us,’ and, ‘What can the Almighty do to us?’
net.JOB_22_18: But it was he who filled their houses with good things— yet the counsel of the wicked was far from me.
net.JOB_22_19: The righteous see their destruction and rejoice; the innocent mock them scornfully, saying,
net.JOB_22_20: ‘Surely our enemies are destroyed, and fire consumes their wealth.’
net.JOB_22_21: “Reconcile yourself with God, and be at peace with him; in this way your prosperity will be good.
net.JOB_22_22: Accept instruction from his mouth and store up his words in your heart.
net.JOB_22_23: If you return to the Almighty, you will be built up; if you remove wicked behavior far from your tent,
net.JOB_22_24: and throw your gold in the dust— your gold of Ophir among the rocks in the ravines—
net.JOB_22_25: then the Almighty himself will be your gold, and the choicest silver for you.
net.JOB_22_26: Surely then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and will lift up your face toward God.
net.JOB_22_27: You will pray to him and he will hear you, and you will fulfill your vows to him.
net.JOB_22_28: Whatever you decide on a matter, it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.
net.JOB_22_29: When people are brought low and you say, ‘Lift them up!’ then he will save the downcast;
net.JOB_22_30: he will deliver even someone who is not innocent, who will escape through the cleanness of your hands.”
net.JOB_23_2: “Even today my complaint is still bitter; his hand is heavy despite my groaning.
net.JOB_23_3: O that I knew where I might find him, that I could come to his place of residence!
net.JOB_23_4: I would lay out my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
net.JOB_23_5: I would know with what words he would answer me, and understand what he would say to me.
net.JOB_23_6: Would he contend with me with great power? No, he would only pay attention to me.
net.JOB_23_7: There an upright person could present his case before him, and I would be delivered forever from my judge. The Inaccessibility and Power of God
net.JOB_23_8: “If I go to the east, he is not there, and to the west, yet I do not perceive him.
net.JOB_23_9: In the north when he is at work, I do not see him; when he turns to the south, I see no trace of him.
net.JOB_23_10: But he knows the pathway that I take; if he tested me, I would come forth like gold.
net.JOB_23_11: My feet have followed his steps closely; I have kept to his way and have not turned aside.
net.JOB_23_12: I have not departed from the commands of his lips; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my allotted portion.
net.JOB_23_13: But he is unchangeable, and who can change him? Whatever he has desired, he does.
net.JOB_23_14: For he fulfills his decree against me, and many such things are his plans.
net.JOB_23_15: That is why I am terrified in his presence; when I consider, I am afraid because of him.
net.JOB_23_16: Indeed, God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me.
net.JOB_23_17: Yet I have not been silent because of the darkness, because of the thick darkness that covered my face.
net.JOB_24_1: “Why are times not appointed by the Almighty? Why do those who know him not see his days?
net.JOB_24_2: Men move boundary stones; they seize the flock and pasture them.
net.JOB_24_3: They drive away the orphan’s donkey; they take the widow’s ox as a pledge.
net.JOB_24_4: They turn the needy from the pathway, and the poor of the land hide themselves together.
net.JOB_24_5: Like wild donkeys in the wilderness, they go out to their labor seeking diligently for food; the arid rift valley provides food for them and for their children.
net.JOB_24_6: They reap fodder in the field, and glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
net.JOB_24_7: They spend the night naked because they lack clothing; they have no covering against the cold.
net.JOB_24_8: They are soaked by mountain rains and huddle in the rocks because they lack shelter.
net.JOB_24_9: The fatherless child is snatched from the breast, the infant of the poor is taken as a pledge.
net.JOB_24_10: They go about naked, without clothing, and go hungry while they carry the sheaves.
net.JOB_24_11: They press out the olive oil between the rows of olive trees; they tread the winepresses while they are thirsty.
net.JOB_24_12: From the city the dying groan, and the wounded cry out for help, but God charges no one with wrongdoing.
net.JOB_24_13: There are those who rebel against the light; they do not know its ways and they do not stay on its paths.
net.JOB_24_14: Before daybreak the murderer rises up; he kills the poor and the needy; in the night he is like a thief.
net.JOB_24_15: And the eye of the adulterer watches for the twilight, thinking, ‘No eye can see me,’ and covers his face with a mask.
net.JOB_24_16: In the dark the robber breaks into houses, but by day they shut themselves in; they do not know the light.
net.JOB_24_17: For all of them, the morning is to them like deep darkness; they are friends with the terrors of darkness.
net.JOB_24_18: “You say, ‘He is foam on the face of the waters; their portion of the land is cursed so that no one goes to their vineyard.
net.JOB_24_19: The drought as well as the heat snatch up the melted snow; so the grave snatches up the sinner.
net.JOB_24_20: The womb forgets him, the worm feasts on him, no longer will he be remembered. Like a tree, wickedness will be broken down.
net.JOB_24_21: He preys on the barren and childless woman, and does not treat the widow well.
net.JOB_24_22: But God drags off the mighty by his power; when God rises up against him, he has no faith in his life.
net.JOB_24_23: God may let them rest in a feeling of security, but he is constantly watching all their ways.
net.JOB_24_24: They are exalted for a little while, and then they are gone, they are brought low like all others, and gathered in, and like a head of grain they are cut off.’
net.JOB_24_25: “If this is not so, who can prove me a liar and reduce my words to nothing?”
net.JOB_25_2: “Dominion and awesome might belong to God; he establishes peace in his heights.
net.JOB_25_3: Can his armies be numbered? On whom does his light not rise?
net.JOB_25_4: How then can a human being be righteous before God? How can one born of a woman be pure?
net.JOB_25_5: If even the moon is not bright, and the stars are not pure as far as he is concerned,
net.JOB_25_6: how much less a mortal man, who is but a maggot — a son of man, who is only a worm!”
net.JOB_26_2: “How you have helped the powerless! How you have saved the person who has no strength!
net.JOB_26_3: How you have advised the one without wisdom, and abundantly revealed your insight!
net.JOB_26_4: To whom did you utter these words? And whose spirit has come forth from your mouth? A Better Description of God’s Greatness
net.JOB_26_5: “The dead tremble — those beneath the waters and all that live in them.
net.JOB_26_6: The underworld is naked before God; the place of destruction lies uncovered.
net.JOB_26_7: He spreads out the northern skies over empty space; he suspends the earth on nothing.
net.JOB_26_8: He locks the waters in his clouds, and the clouds do not burst with the weight of them.
net.JOB_26_9: He conceals the face of the full moon, shrouding it with his clouds.
net.JOB_26_10: He marks out the horizon on the surface of the waters as a boundary between light and darkness.
net.JOB_26_11: The pillars of the heavens tremble and are amazed at his rebuke.
net.JOB_26_12: By his power he stills the sea; by his wisdom he cut Rahab the great sea monster to pieces.
net.JOB_26_13: By his breath the skies became fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
net.JOB_26_14: Indeed, these are but the outer fringes of his ways! How faint is the whisper we hear of him! But who can understand the thunder of his power?”
net.JOB_27_1: And Job took up his discourse again:
net.JOB_27_2: “As surely as God lives, who has denied me justice, the Almighty, who has made my life bitter —
net.JOB_27_3: for while my spirit is still in me, and the breath from God is in my nostrils,
net.JOB_27_4: my lips will not speak wickedness, and my tongue will whisper no deceit.
net.JOB_27_5: I will never declare that you three are in the right; until I die, I will not set aside my integrity!
net.JOB_27_6: I will maintain my righteousness and never let it go; my conscience will not reproach me for as long as I live. The Condition of the Wicked
net.JOB_27_7: “May my enemy be like the wicked, my adversary like the unrighteous.
net.JOB_27_8: For what hope does the godless have when he is cut off, when God takes away his life?
net.JOB_27_9: Does God listen to his cry when distress overtakes him?
net.JOB_27_10: Will he find delight in the Almighty? Will he call out to God at all times?
net.JOB_27_11: I will teach you about the power of God; what is on the Almighty’s mind I will not conceal.
net.JOB_27_12: If you yourselves have all seen this, Why in the world do you continue this meaningless talk?
net.JOB_27_13: This is the portion of the wicked man allotted by God, the inheritance that evildoers receive from the Almighty.
net.JOB_27_14: If his children increase—it is for the sword! His offspring never have enough to eat.
net.JOB_27_15: Those who survive him are buried by the plague, and their widows do not mourn for them.
net.JOB_27_16: If he piles up silver like dust and stores up clothing like mounds of clay,
net.JOB_27_17: what he stores up a righteous man will wear, and an innocent man will inherit his silver.
net.JOB_27_18: The house he builds is as fragile as a moth’s cocoon, like a hut that a watchman has made.
net.JOB_27_19: He goes to bed wealthy, but will do so no more. When he opens his eyes, it is all gone.
net.JOB_27_20: Terrors overwhelm him like a flood; at night a whirlwind carries him off.
net.JOB_27_21: The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
net.JOB_27_22: It hurls itself against him without pity as he flees headlong from its power.
net.JOB_27_23: It claps its hands at him in derision and hisses him away from his place.
net.JOB_28_1: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place where gold is refined.
net.JOB_28_2: Iron is taken from the ground, and rock is poured out as copper.
net.JOB_28_3: Man puts an end to the darkness; he searches the farthest recesses for the ore in the deepest darkness.
net.JOB_28_4: Far from where people live he sinks a shaft, in places travelers have long forgotten, far from other people he dangles and sways.
net.JOB_28_5: The earth, from which food comes, is overturned below as though by fire;
net.JOB_28_6: a place whose stones are sapphires that contain dust of gold;
net.JOB_28_7: a hidden path no bird of prey knows— no falcon’s eye has spotted it.
net.JOB_28_8: Proud beasts have not set foot on it, and no lion has passed along it.
net.JOB_28_9: On the flinty rock man has set to work with his hand; he has overturned mountains at their bases.
net.JOB_28_10: He has cut out channels through the rocks; his eyes have spotted every precious thing.
net.JOB_28_11: He has searched the sources of the rivers and what was hidden he has brought into the light. No Price Can Buy Wisdom
net.JOB_28_12: “But wisdom—where can it be found? Where is the place of understanding?
net.JOB_28_13: Mankind does not know its place; it cannot be found in the land of the living.
net.JOB_28_14: The deep says, ‘It is not with me.’ And the sea says, ‘It is not with me.’
net.JOB_28_15: Fine gold cannot be given in exchange for it, nor can its price be weighed out in silver.
net.JOB_28_16: It cannot be measured out for purchase with the gold of Ophir, with precious onyx or sapphires.
net.JOB_28_17: Neither gold nor crystal can be compared with it, nor can a vase of gold match its worth.
net.JOB_28_18: Of coral and jasper no mention will be made; the price of wisdom is more than pearls.
net.JOB_28_19: The topaz of Cush cannot be compared with it; it cannot be purchased with pure gold. God Alone Has Wisdom
net.JOB_28_20: “But wisdom—where does it come from? Where is the place of understanding?
net.JOB_28_21: For it has been hidden from the eyes of every living creature, and from the birds of the sky it has been concealed.
net.JOB_28_22: Destruction and Death say, ‘With our ears we have heard a rumor about where it can be found.’
net.JOB_28_23: God understands the way to it, and he alone knows its place.
net.JOB_28_24: For he looks to the ends of the earth and observes everything under the heavens.
net.JOB_28_25: When he made the force of the wind and measured the waters with a gauge,
net.JOB_28_26: when he imposed a limit for the rain, and a path for the thunderstorm,
net.JOB_28_27: then he looked at wisdom and assessed its value; he established it and examined it closely.
net.JOB_28_28: And he said to mankind, ‘The fear of the Lord —that is wisdom, and to turn away from evil is understanding.’”
net.JOB_29_2: “O that I could be as I was in the months now gone, in the days when God watched over me,
net.JOB_29_3: when he caused his lamp to shine upon my head, and by his light I walked through darkness;
net.JOB_29_4: just as I was in my most productive time, when God’s intimate friendship was experienced in my tent,
net.JOB_29_5: when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me;
net.JOB_29_6: when my steps were bathed with butter and the rock poured out for me streams of olive oil!
net.JOB_29_7: When I went out to the city gate and secured my seat in the public square,
net.JOB_29_8: the young men would see me and step aside, and the old men would get up and remain standing;
net.JOB_29_9: the chief men refrained from talking and covered their mouths with their hands;
net.JOB_29_10: the voices of the nobles fell silent, and their tongues stuck to the roof of their mouths. Job’s Benevolence
net.JOB_29_11: “As soon as the ear heard these things, it blessed me, and when the eye saw them, it bore witness to me,
net.JOB_29_12: for I rescued the poor who cried out for help, and the orphan who had no one to assist him;
net.JOB_29_13: the blessing of the dying man descended on me, and I made the widow’s heart rejoice;
net.JOB_29_14: I put on righteousness and it clothed me, my just dealing was like a robe and a turban;
net.JOB_29_15: I was eyes for the blind and feet for the lame;
net.JOB_29_16: I was a father to the needy, and I investigated the case of the person I did not know;
net.JOB_29_17: I broke the fangs of the wicked, and made him drop his prey from his teeth. Job’s Confidence
net.JOB_29_18: “Then I thought, ‘I will die in my own home, my days as numerous as the grains of sand.
net.JOB_29_19: My roots reach the water, and the dew lies on my branches all night long.
net.JOB_29_20: My glory will always be fresh in me, and my bow ever new in my hand.’ Job’s Reputation
net.JOB_29_21: “People listened to me and waited silently; they kept silent for my advice.
net.JOB_29_22: After I had spoken, they did not respond; my words fell on them drop by drop.
net.JOB_29_23: They waited for me as people wait for the rain, and they opened their mouths as for the spring rains.
net.JOB_29_24: If I smiled at them, they hardly believed it; and they did not cause the light of my face to darken.
net.JOB_29_25: I chose the way for them and sat as their chief; I lived like a king among his troops; I was like one who comforts mourners.
net.JOB_30_1: “But now they mock me, those who are younger than I, whose fathers I disdained too much to put with my sheep dogs.
net.JOB_30_2: Moreover, the strength of their hands— what use was it to me? Those whose strength had perished,
net.JOB_30_3: gaunt with want and hunger, they would roam the parched land, by night a desolate waste.
net.JOB_30_4: By the brush they would gather herbs from the salt marshes, and the root of the broom tree was their food.
net.JOB_30_5: They were banished from the community — people shouted at them as they would shout at thieves —
net.JOB_30_6: so that they had to live in the dry stream beds, in the holes of the ground, and among the rocks.
net.JOB_30_7: They brayed like animals among the bushes and were huddled together under the nettles.
net.JOB_30_8: Sons of senseless and nameless people, they were driven out of the land with whips. Job’s Indignities
net.JOB_30_9: “And now I have become their taunt song; I have become a byword among them.
net.JOB_30_10: They detest me and maintain their distance; they do not hesitate to spit in my face.
net.JOB_30_11: Because God has untied my tent cord and afflicted me, people throw off all restraint in my presence.
net.JOB_30_12: On my right the young rabble rise up; they drive me from place to place, and build up siege ramps against me.
net.JOB_30_13: They destroy my path; they succeed in destroying me without anyone assisting them.
net.JOB_30_14: They come in as through a wide breach; amid the crash they come rolling in.
net.JOB_30_15: Terrors are turned loose on me; they drive away my honor like the wind, and as a cloud my deliverance has passed away. Job’s Despondency
net.JOB_30_16: “And now my soul pours itself out within me; days of suffering take hold of me.
net.JOB_30_17: Night pierces my bones; my gnawing pains never cease.
net.JOB_30_18: With great power God grasps my clothing; he binds me like the collar of my tunic.
net.JOB_30_19: He has flung me into the mud, and I have come to resemble dust and ashes.
net.JOB_30_20: I cry out to you, but you do not answer me; I stand up, and you only look at me.
net.JOB_30_21: You have become cruel to me; with the strength of your hand you attack me.
net.JOB_30_22: You pick me up on the wind and make me ride on it; you toss me about in the storm.
net.JOB_30_23: I know that you are bringing me to death, to the meeting place for all the living. The Contrast With the Past
net.JOB_30_24: “Surely one does not stretch out his hand against a broken man when he cries for help in his distress.
net.JOB_30_25: Have I not wept for the unfortunate? Was not my soul grieved for the poor?
net.JOB_30_26: But when I hoped for good, trouble came; when I expected light, then darkness came.
net.JOB_30_27: My heart is in turmoil unceasingly; the days of my affliction confront me.
net.JOB_30_28: I go about blackened, but not by the sun; in the assembly I stand up and cry for help.
net.JOB_30_29: I have become a brother to jackals and a companion of ostriches.
net.JOB_30_30: My skin has turned dark on me; my body is hot with fever.
net.JOB_30_31: My harp is used for mourning and my flute for the sound of weeping.
net.JOB_31_1: “I made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I entertain thoughts against a virgin?
net.JOB_31_2: What then would be one’s lot from God above, one’s heritage from the Almighty on high?
net.JOB_31_3: Is it not misfortune for the unjust, and disaster for those who work iniquity?
net.JOB_31_4: Does he not see my ways and count all my steps?
net.JOB_31_5: If I have walked in falsehood, and if my foot has hastened to deceit—
net.JOB_31_6: let him weigh me with honest scales; then God will discover my integrity.
net.JOB_31_7: If my footsteps have strayed from the way, if my heart has gone after my eyes, or if anything has defiled my hands,
net.JOB_31_8: then let me sow and let another eat, and let my crops be uprooted.
net.JOB_31_9: If my heart has been enticed by a woman, and I have lain in wait at my neighbor’s door,
net.JOB_31_10: then let my wife turn the millstone for another man, and may other men commit adultery with her.
net.JOB_31_11: For I would have committed a shameful act, an iniquity to be judged.
net.JOB_31_12: For it is a fire that devours even to Destruction, and it would uproot all my harvest.
net.JOB_31_13: “If I have disregarded the right of my male servants or my female servants when they disputed with me,
net.JOB_31_14: then what will I do when God confronts me in judgment; when he intervenes, how will I respond to him?
net.JOB_31_15: Did not the one who made me in the womb make them? Did not the same one form us in the womb?
net.JOB_31_16: If I have refused to give the poor what they desired, or caused the eyes of the widow to fail,
net.JOB_31_17: If I ate my morsel of bread myself, and did not share any of it with orphans —
net.JOB_31_18: but from my youth I raised the orphan like a father, and from my mother’s womb I guided the widow —
net.JOB_31_19: If I have seen anyone about to perish for lack of clothing, or a poor man without a coat,
net.JOB_31_20: whose heart did not bless me as he warmed himself with the fleece of my sheep,
net.JOB_31_21: if I have raised my hand to vote against the orphan, when I saw my support in the court,
net.JOB_31_22: then let my arm fall from the shoulder, let my arm be broken off at the socket.
net.JOB_31_23: For the calamity from God was a terror to me, and by reason of his majesty I was powerless.
net.JOB_31_24: “If I have put my confidence in gold or said to pure gold, ‘You are my security!’
net.JOB_31_25: if I have rejoiced because of the extent of my wealth, or because of the great wealth my hand had gained,
net.JOB_31_26: if I looked at the sun when it was shining, and the moon advancing as a precious thing,
net.JOB_31_27: so that my heart was secretly enticed, and my hand threw them a kiss from my mouth,
net.JOB_31_28: then this also would be iniquity to be judged, for I would have been false to God above.
net.JOB_31_29: If I have rejoiced over the misfortune of my enemy or exulted because calamity found him—
net.JOB_31_30: I have not even permitted my mouth to sin by asking for his life through a curse—
net.JOB_31_31: if the members of my household have never said, ‘If only there were someone who has not been satisfied from Job’s meat!’—
net.JOB_31_32: But no stranger had to spend the night outside, for I opened my doors to the traveler —
net.JOB_31_33: if I have covered my transgressions as men do, by hiding iniquity in my heart,
net.JOB_31_34: because I was terrified of the great multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I remained silent and would not go outdoors— Job’s Appeal
net.JOB_31_35: “If only I had someone to hear me! Here is my signature — let the Almighty answer me! If only I had an indictment that my accuser had written.
net.JOB_31_36: Surely I would wear it proudly on my shoulder, I would bind it on me like a crown;
net.JOB_31_37: I would give him an accounting of my steps; like a prince I would draw near to him. Job’s Final Solemn Oath
net.JOB_31_38: “If my land cried out against me and all its furrows wept together,
net.JOB_31_39: if I have eaten its produce without paying, or caused the death of its owners,
net.JOB_31_40: then let thorns sprout up in place of wheat, and in place of barley, noxious weeds.” The words of Job are ended.
net.JOB_32_1: So these three men refused to answer Job further, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
net.JOB_32_2: Then Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, became very angry. He was angry with Job for justifying himself rather than God.
net.JOB_32_3: With Job’s three friends he was also angry, because they could not find an answer, and so declared Job guilty.
net.JOB_32_4: Now Elihu had waited before speaking to Job, because the others were older than he was.
net.JOB_32_5: But when Elihu saw that the three men had no further reply, he became very angry. Elihu Claims Wisdom
net.JOB_32_6: So Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite spoke up: “I am young, but you are elderly; that is why I was fearful, and afraid to explain to you what I know.
net.JOB_32_7: I said to myself, ‘Age should speak, and length of years should make wisdom known.’
net.JOB_32_8: But it is a spirit in people, the breath of the Almighty, that makes them understand.
net.JOB_32_9: It is not the aged who are wise, nor old men who understand what is right.
net.JOB_32_10: Therefore I say, ‘Listen to me. I, even I, will explain what I know.’
net.JOB_32_11: Look, I waited for you to speak; I listened closely to your wise thoughts, while you were searching for words.
net.JOB_32_12: Now I was paying you close attention, yet there was no one proving Job wrong, not one of you was answering his statements.
net.JOB_32_13: So do not say, ‘We have found wisdom. God will refute him, not man.’
net.JOB_32_14: Job has not directed his words to me, and so I will not reply to him with your arguments. Job’s Friends Failed to Answer
net.JOB_32_15: “They are dismayed and cannot answer anymore; they have nothing left to say.
net.JOB_32_16: And I have waited. But because they do not speak, because they stand there and answer no more,
net.JOB_32_17: I too will answer my part, I too will explain what I know.
net.JOB_32_18: For I am full of words, and the spirit within me constrains me.
net.JOB_32_19: Inside I am like wine that has no outlet, like new wineskins ready to burst!
net.JOB_32_20: I will speak, so that I may find relief; I will open my lips, so that I may answer.
net.JOB_32_21: I will not show partiality to any person, nor will I confer a title on anyone.
net.JOB_32_22: For I do not know how to give honorary titles, if I did, my Creator would quickly do away with me.
net.JOB_33_1: “But now, O Job, listen to my words, and hear everything I have to say.
net.JOB_33_2: See now, I have opened my mouth; my tongue in my mouth has spoken.
net.JOB_33_3: My words come from the uprightness of my heart, and my lips will utter knowledge sincerely.
net.JOB_33_4: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
net.JOB_33_5: Reply to me, if you can; set your arguments in order before me and take your stand.
net.JOB_33_6: Look, I am just like you in relation to God; I too have been molded from clay.
net.JOB_33_7: Therefore no fear of me should terrify you, nor should my pressure be heavy on you. Elihu Rejects Job’s Plea of Innocence
net.JOB_33_8: “Indeed, you have said in my hearing (I heard the sound of the words!):
net.JOB_33_9: ‘I am pure, without transgression; I am clean and have no iniquity.
net.JOB_33_10: Yet God finds occasions with me; he regards me as his enemy.
net.JOB_33_11: He puts my feet in shackles; he watches closely all my paths.’
net.JOB_33_12: Now in this, you are not right—I answer you, for God is greater than a human being.
net.JOB_33_13: Why do you contend against him, that he does not answer all a person’s words? Elihu Disagrees With Job’s View of God
net.JOB_33_14: “For God speaks, the first time in one way, the second time in another, though a person does not perceive it.
net.JOB_33_15: In a dream, a night vision, when deep sleep falls on people as they sleep in their beds.
net.JOB_33_16: Then he gives a revelation to people, and terrifies them with warnings,
net.JOB_33_17: to turn a person from his sin, and to cover a person’s pride.
net.JOB_33_18: He spares a person’s life from corruption, his very life from crossing over the river.
net.JOB_33_19: Or a person is chastened by pain on his bed, and with the continual strife of his bones,
net.JOB_33_20: so that his life loathes food, and his soul rejects appetizing fare.
net.JOB_33_21: His flesh wastes away from sight, and his bones, which were not seen, are easily visible.
net.JOB_33_22: He draws near to the place of corruption, and his life to the messengers of death.
net.JOB_33_23: If there is an angel beside him, one mediator out of a thousand, to tell a person what constitutes his uprightness;
net.JOB_33_24: and if God is gracious to him and says, ‘Spare him from going down to the place of corruption, I have found a ransom for him,’
net.JOB_33_25: then his flesh is restored like a youth’s; he returns to the days of his youthful vigor.
net.JOB_33_26: He entreats God, and God delights in him, he sees God’s face with rejoicing, and God restores to him his righteousness.
net.JOB_33_27: That person sings to others, saying: ‘I have sinned and falsified what is right, but I was not punished according to what I deserved.
net.JOB_33_28: He redeemed my life from going down to the place of corruption, and my life sees the light!’ Elihu’s Appeal to Job
net.JOB_33_29: “Indeed, God does all these things, twice, three times, in his dealings with a person,
net.JOB_33_30: to turn back his life from the place of corruption, that he may be enlightened with the light of life.
net.JOB_33_31: Pay attention, Job—listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
net.JOB_33_32: If you have any words, reply to me; speak, for I want to justify you.
net.JOB_33_33: If not, you listen to me; be silent, and I will teach you wisdom.”
net.JOB_34_2: “Listen to my words, you wise men; hear me, you learned men.
net.JOB_34_3: For the ear assesses words as the mouth tastes food.
net.JOB_34_4: Let us evaluate for ourselves what is right; let us come to know among ourselves what is good.
net.JOB_34_5: For Job says, ‘I am innocent, but God turns away my right.
net.JOB_34_6: Concerning my right, should I lie? My wound is incurable, although I am without transgression.’
net.JOB_34_7: Who is there like Job, who drinks derision like water?
net.JOB_34_8: He goes about in company with evildoers, he goes along with wicked men.
net.JOB_34_9: For he says, ‘It does not profit a man when he makes his delight with God.’ God is Not Unjust
net.JOB_34_10: “Therefore, listen to me, you men of understanding. Far be it from God to do wickedness, from the Almighty to do evil.
net.JOB_34_11: For he repays a person for his work, and according to the conduct of a person, he causes the consequences to find him.
net.JOB_34_12: Indeed, in truth, God does not act wickedly, and the Almighty does not pervert justice.
net.JOB_34_13: Who entrusted to him the earth? And who put him over the whole world?
net.JOB_34_14: If God were to set his heart on it, and gather in his spirit and his breath,
net.JOB_34_15: all flesh would perish together and human beings would return to dust. God Is Impartial and Omniscient
net.JOB_34_16: “If you have understanding, listen to this, hear what I have to say.
net.JOB_34_17: Do you really think that one who hates justice can govern? And will you declare guilty the supremely Righteous One,
net.JOB_34_18: who says to a king, ‘Worthless man,’ and to nobles, ‘Wicked men,’
net.JOB_34_19: who shows no partiality to princes, and does not take note of the rich more than the poor, because all of them are the work of his hands?
net.JOB_34_20: In a moment they die, in the middle of the night, people are shaken and they pass away. The mighty are removed effortlessly.
net.JOB_34_21: For his eyes are on the ways of an individual, he observes all a person’s steps.
net.JOB_34_22: There is no darkness, and no deep darkness, where evildoers can hide themselves.
net.JOB_34_23: For he does not still consider a person, that he should come before God in judgment.
net.JOB_34_24: He shatters the great without inquiry, and sets up others in their place.
net.JOB_34_25: Therefore, he knows their deeds, he overthrows them in the night and they are crushed.
net.JOB_34_26: He strikes them for their wickedness, in a place where people can see,
net.JOB_34_27: because they have turned away from following him, and have not understood any of his ways,
net.JOB_34_28: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come before him, so that he hears the cry of the needy.
net.JOB_34_29: But if God is quiet, who can condemn him? If he hides his face, then who can see him? Yet he is over the individual and the nation alike,
net.JOB_34_30: so that the godless man should not rule, and not lay snares for the people. Job Is Foolish to Rebel
net.JOB_34_31: “Has anyone said to God, ‘I have endured chastisement, but I will not act wrongly any more;
net.JOB_34_32: teach me what I cannot see; if I have done evil, I will do so no more’?
net.JOB_34_33: Is it your opinion that God should recompense it, because you reject this? But you must choose, and not I, so tell us what you know.
net.JOB_34_34: Men of understanding say to me— any wise man listening to me says—
net.JOB_34_35: that Job speaks without knowledge and his words are without understanding.
net.JOB_34_36: But Job will be tested to the end, because his answers are like those of wicked men.
net.JOB_34_37: For he adds transgression to his sin; in our midst he claps his hands, and multiplies his words against God.”
net.JOB_35_2: “Do you think this to be just when you say, ‘My right before God’?
net.JOB_35_3: But you say, ‘What will it profit you,’ and, ‘What do I gain by not sinning?’
net.JOB_35_4: I will reply to you, and to your friends with you.
net.JOB_35_5: Gaze at the heavens and see; consider the clouds, which are higher than you.
net.JOB_35_6: If you sin, how does it affect God? If your transgressions are many, what does it do to him?
net.JOB_35_7: If you are righteous, what do you give to God, or what does he receive from your hand?
net.JOB_35_8: Your wickedness affects only a person like yourself, and your righteousness only other people.
net.JOB_35_9: “People cry out because of the excess of oppression; they cry out for help because of the power of the mighty.
net.JOB_35_10: But no one says, ‘Where is God, my Creator, who gives songs in the night,
net.JOB_35_11: who teaches us more than the wild animals of the earth, and makes us wiser than the birds of the sky?’
net.JOB_35_12: Then they cry out—but he does not answer— because of the arrogance of the wicked.
net.JOB_35_13: Surely it is an empty cry —God does not hear it; the Almighty does not take notice of it.
net.JOB_35_14: How much less, then, when you say that you do not perceive him, that the case is before him and you are waiting for him!
net.JOB_35_15: And further, when you say that his anger does not punish, and that he does not know transgression!
net.JOB_35_16: So Job opens his mouth to no purpose; without knowledge he multiplies words.”
net.JOB_36_2: “Be patient with me a little longer and I will instruct you, for I still have words to speak on God’s behalf.
net.JOB_36_3: With my knowledge I will speak comprehensively, and to my Creator I will ascribe righteousness.
net.JOB_36_4: For in truth, my words are not false; it is one complete in knowledge who is with you.
net.JOB_36_5: Indeed, God is mighty; and he does not despise people, he is mighty, and firm in his intent.
net.JOB_36_6: He does not allow the wicked to live, but he gives justice to the poor.
net.JOB_36_7: He does not take his eyes off the righteous; but with kings on the throne he seats the righteous and exalts them forever.
net.JOB_36_8: But if they are bound in chains, and held captive by the cords of affliction,
net.JOB_36_9: then he reveals to them what they have done, and their transgressions, that they were behaving proudly.
net.JOB_36_10: And he reveals this for correction, and says that they must turn from evil.
net.JOB_36_11: If they obey and serve him, they live out their days in prosperity and their years in pleasantness.
net.JOB_36_12: But if they refuse to listen, they pass over the river of death, and expire without knowledge.
net.JOB_36_13: The godless at heart nourish anger, they do not cry out even when he binds them.
net.JOB_36_14: They die in their youth, and their life ends among the male cultic prostitutes.
net.JOB_36_15: He delivers the afflicted by their afflictions, he reveals himself to them by their suffering.
net.JOB_36_16: And surely, he drew you from the mouth of distress, to a wide place, unrestricted, and to the comfort of your table filled with rich food.
net.JOB_36_17: But now you are preoccupied with the judgment due the wicked, judgment and justice take hold of you.
net.JOB_36_18: Be careful that no one entices you with riches; do not let a large bribe turn you aside.
net.JOB_36_19: Would your wealth sustain you, so that you would not be in distress, even all your mighty efforts?
net.JOB_36_20: Do not long for the cover of night to drag people away from their homes.
net.JOB_36_21: Take heed, do not turn to evil, for because of this you have been tested by affliction.
net.JOB_36_22: Indeed, God is exalted in his power; who is a teacher like him?
net.JOB_36_23: Who has prescribed his ways for him? Or said to him, ‘You have done what is wicked’?
net.JOB_36_24: Remember to extol his work, which people have praised in song.
net.JOB_36_25: All humanity has seen it; people gaze on it from afar. The Work and Wisdom of God
net.JOB_36_26: “Yes, God is great—beyond our knowledge! The number of his years is unsearchable.
net.JOB_36_27: He draws up drops of water; they distill the rain into its mist,
net.JOB_36_28: which the clouds pour down and shower on humankind abundantly.
net.JOB_36_29: Who can understand the spreading of the clouds, the thunderings of his pavilion?
net.JOB_36_30: See how he scattered his lightning about him; he has covered the depths of the sea.
net.JOB_36_31: It is by these that he judges the nations and supplies food in abundance.
net.JOB_36_32: With his hands he covers the lightning, and directs it against its target.
net.JOB_36_33: His thunder announces the coming storm, the cattle also, concerning the storm’s approach.
net.JOB_37_1: At this also my heart pounds and leaps from its place.
net.JOB_37_2: Listen carefully to the thunder of his voice, to the rumbling that proceeds from his mouth.
net.JOB_37_3: Under the whole heaven he lets it go, even his lightning to the far corners of the earth.
net.JOB_37_4: After that a voice roars; he thunders with an exalted voice, and he does not hold back his lightning bolts when his voice is heard.
net.JOB_37_5: God thunders with his voice in marvelous ways; he does great things beyond our understanding.
net.JOB_37_6: For to the snow he says, ‘Fall to earth,’ and to the torrential rains, ‘Pour down.’
net.JOB_37_7: He causes everyone to stop working, so that all people may know his work.
net.JOB_37_8: The wild animals go to their lairs, and in their dens they remain.
net.JOB_37_9: A tempest blows out from its chamber, icy cold from the driving winds.
net.JOB_37_10: The breath of God produces ice, and the breadth of the waters freeze solid.
net.JOB_37_11: He loads the clouds with moisture; he scatters his lightning through the clouds.
net.JOB_37_12: The clouds go round in circles, wheeling about according to his plans, to carry out all that he commands them over the face of the whole inhabited world.
net.JOB_37_13: Whether it is for punishment, or for his land, or for mercy, he causes it to find its mark.
net.JOB_37_14: “Pay attention to this, Job! Stand still and consider the wonders God works.
net.JOB_37_15: Do you know how God commands them, how he makes lightning flash in his storm cloud?
net.JOB_37_16: Do you know about the balancing of the clouds, that wondrous activity of him who is perfect in knowledge?
net.JOB_37_17: You, whose garments are hot when the earth is still because of the south wind,
net.JOB_37_18: will you, with him, spread out the clouds, solid as a mirror of molten metal?
net.JOB_37_19: Tell us what we should say to him. We cannot prepare a case because of the darkness.
net.JOB_37_20: Should he be informed that I want to speak? If a man speaks, surely he will be swallowed up!
net.JOB_37_21: But now, the sun cannot be looked at — it is bright in the skies— after a wind passed and swept the clouds away.
net.JOB_37_22: From the north he comes in golden splendor; around God is awesome majesty.
net.JOB_37_23: As for the Almighty, we cannot attain to him! He is great in power, but justice and abundant righteousness he does not oppress.
net.JOB_37_24: Therefore people fear him, for he does not regard all the wise in heart.”
net.JOB_38_1: Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind:
net.JOB_38_2: “Who is this who darkens counsel with words without knowledge?
net.JOB_38_3: Get ready for a difficult task like a man; I will question you and you will inform me. God’s questions to Job
net.JOB_38_4: “Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me, if you possess understanding.
net.JOB_38_5: Who set its measurements—if you know— or who stretched a measuring line across it?
net.JOB_38_6: On what were its bases set, or who laid its cornerstone—
net.JOB_38_7: when the morning stars sang in chorus, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
net.JOB_38_8: “Who shut up the sea with doors when it burst forth, coming out of the womb,
net.JOB_38_9: when I made the storm clouds its garment, and thick darkness its swaddling band,
net.JOB_38_10: when I prescribed its limits, and set in place its bolts and doors,
net.JOB_38_11: when I said, ‘To here you may come and no farther, here your proud waves will be confined’?
net.JOB_38_12: Have you ever in your life commanded the morning, or made the dawn know its place,
net.JOB_38_13: that it might seize the corners of the earth, and shake the wicked out of it?
net.JOB_38_14: The earth takes shape like clay under a seal; its features are dyed like a garment.
net.JOB_38_15: Then from the wicked the light is withheld, and the arm raised in violence is broken.
net.JOB_38_16: Have you gone to the springs that fill the sea, or walked about in the recesses of the deep?
net.JOB_38_17: Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Have you seen the gates of deepest darkness?
net.JOB_38_18: Have you considered the vast expanses of the earth? Tell me, if you know it all.
net.JOB_38_19: “In what direction does light reside, and darkness, where is its place,
net.JOB_38_20: that you may take them to their borders and perceive the pathways to their homes?
net.JOB_38_21: You know, for you were born before them; and the number of your days is great!
net.JOB_38_22: Have you entered the storehouse of the snow, or seen the armory of the hail,
net.JOB_38_23: which I reserve for the time of trouble, for the day of war and battle?
net.JOB_38_24: In what direction is lightning dispersed, or the east winds scattered over the earth?
net.JOB_38_25: Who carves out a channel for the heavy rains, and a path for the rumble of thunder,
net.JOB_38_26: to cause it to rain on an uninhabited land, a wilderness where there are no human beings,
net.JOB_38_27: to satisfy a devastated and desolate land, and to cause it to sprout with vegetation?
net.JOB_38_28: Does the rain have a father, or who has fathered the drops of the dew?
net.JOB_38_29: From whose womb does the ice emerge, and the frost from the sky, who gives birth to it,
net.JOB_38_30: when the waters become hard like stone, when the surface of the deep is frozen solid?
net.JOB_38_31: Can you tie the bands of the Pleiades, or release the cords of Orion?
net.JOB_38_32: Can you lead out the constellations in their seasons, or guide the Bear with its cubs?
net.JOB_38_33: Do you know the laws of the heavens, or can you set up their rule over the earth?
net.JOB_38_34: Can you raise your voice to the clouds so that a flood of water covers you?
net.JOB_38_35: Can you send out lightning bolts, and they go? Will they say to you, ‘Here we are’?
net.JOB_38_36: Who has put wisdom in the heart, or has imparted understanding to the mind?
net.JOB_38_37: Who by wisdom can count the clouds, and who can tip over the water jars of heaven,
net.JOB_38_38: when the dust hardens into a mass, and the clumps of earth stick together?
net.JOB_38_39: “Do you hunt prey for the lioness, and satisfy the appetite of the lions
net.JOB_38_40: when they crouch in their dens, when they wait in ambush in the thicket?
net.JOB_38_41: Who prepares prey for the raven, when its young cry out to God and wander about for lack of food?
net.JOB_39_1: “Are you acquainted with the way the mountain goats give birth? Do you watch as the wild deer give birth to their young?
net.JOB_39_2: Do you count the months they must fulfill, and do you know the time they give birth?
net.JOB_39_3: They crouch, they bear their young, they bring forth the offspring they have carried.
net.JOB_39_4: Their young grow strong, and grow up in the open; they go off, and do not return to them.
net.JOB_39_5: Who let the wild donkey go free? Who released the bonds of the donkey,
net.JOB_39_6: to whom I appointed the arid rift valley for its home, the salt wastes as its dwelling place?
net.JOB_39_7: It scorns the tumult in the town; it does not hear the shouts of a driver.
net.JOB_39_8: It ranges the hills as its pasture, and searches after every green plant.
net.JOB_39_9: Is the wild ox willing to be your servant? Will it spend the night at your feeding trough?
net.JOB_39_10: Can you bind the wild ox to a furrow with its rope, will it till the valleys, following after you?
net.JOB_39_11: Will you rely on it because its strength is great? Will you commit your labor to it?
net.JOB_39_12: Can you count on it to bring in your grain, and gather the grain to your threshing floor?
net.JOB_39_13: “The wings of the ostrich flap with joy, but are they the pinions and plumage of a stork?
net.JOB_39_14: For she leaves her eggs on the ground, and lets them be warmed on the soil.
net.JOB_39_15: She forgets that a foot might crush them, or that a wild animal might trample them.
net.JOB_39_16: She is harsh with her young, as if they were not hers; she is unconcerned about the uselessness of her labor.
net.JOB_39_17: For God deprived her of wisdom, and did not impart understanding to her.
net.JOB_39_18: But as soon as she springs up, she laughs at the horse and its rider.
